---
title: "Network Analysis of Bad Movies"
date: 2026-03-27
categories: [Network Science, Movies]
author: Wesley Stone, Bethany Wang
image: genrenetwork.png
---

In this project we performed a network analysis of bad movies, defined as having an IMDB rating of 2/10 or less and at least 10 total ratings. From an initial dataset of 86,000 this left us 871 total films. To create our networks, we scraped cast and crew data, and used the built-in genre variable, giving us two different lenses to peer through. From the personnel perspective, we did not find large communities of people involved with multiple films, but rather isolated clusters involved in one or two, suggesting that most bad movies are the product of random variation over time rather than coming from a core set of bad actors and directors. Our genre analysis did observe several distinct communities, which led us to conclude that bad movies are often a failed combination of action, horror and sci-fi.

[View on Overleaf](https://www.overleaf.com/read/YOUR_TOKEN){.btn .btn-primary}
